name: Black Pepper
summary: The everyday spice whose compound piperine dramatically boosts how much of turmeric's curcumin — and other compounds — your body absorbs.

benefits.mechanism: Black pepper's active compound, piperine, blocks enzymes and transporters that would otherwise break down or expel curcumin before it's absorbed — sharply raising how much reaches the bloodstream. Trials pairing curcumin with piperine show meaningful effects (for example on glycemic markers) that plain curcumin often can't achieve.

citations.title: Effects of combination of curcumin and piperine supplementation on glycemic profile in patients with prediabetes and type 2 diabetes mellitus: a systematic review and meta-analysis (Widjanarko et al., J ASEAN Fed Endocr Soc 2024)

surprisingFacts.text: A pinch of black pepper is why turmeric lattes and curry work: piperine can increase curcumin absorption by up to ~20-fold, turning a poorly-absorbed pigment into something the body can actually use.

cautions.effect: Piperine is the reason pepper boosts turmeric's absorption — but it does the same to some medicines, raising blood levels of drugs you did not intend to boost. Large amounts can also irritate the gut.

citations.title: Influence of piperine on the pharmacokinetics of curcumin in animals and human volunteers (Shoba et al., Planta Med 1998)
